zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Borthwick於1413年出生在蘇格蘭米德洛迪安波爾維其可時，她的父親William Borthwick 2nd of Borthwick22歲，母親Beatrice Sinclair24歲。她和Sir John Maxwell of Calderwood至少有1個兒子和3個女兒。她去世於1464年6月5日在英國蘇格蘭哈丁頓史爾武坡 堂 本 薩爾圖享年51歲。

nameMeanings.name: Borthwick
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from the old barony of Borthwick, formerly Loquhariot (Midlothian) and possibly also from Borthwick (Roxburghshire). The placenames are from Old English bord ‘board, table’ + wīc ‘outlying dairy farm’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Borthwick was born in 1413, in Borthwick, Midlothian, Scotland, her father, William Borthwick 2nd of Borthwick, was 22 and her mother, Beatrice Sinclair, was 24. She had at least 1 son and 3 daughters with Sir John Maxwell of Calderwood. She died on 5 June 1464, in Upper Town of Saltoun, Haddingtonshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 51.

Elle a eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles avec Sir John Maxwell of Calderwood. Elle est décédée le 5 juin 1464, à Upper Town of Saltoun, Haddingtonshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 51 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Borthwick(이)가 1413년 스컷랜드 미들로스이언 볼스윅에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Borthwick 2nd of Borthwick(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Beatrice Sinclair(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 Sir John Maxwell of Calderwood(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1464년 6월 5일 영국 스코틀랜드 하딩토실르 업퍼 타운 프 살토우에서 51세의 나이로 사망했다.
